title: Rigid heddle scarf with pickup stick
subtitle: Weaving a 20 x 150 cm scarf with a pickup stick spot pattern on a rigid heddle loom
excerpt: The pickup stick adds a third shed to the rigid heddle loom by manually selecting warp threads in the down-shed position. Turning the stick on its edge opens a pattern shed that lifts those threads to produce small floats on the surface of the cloth. This scarf uses a simple alternating spot pattern worked over a plain-weave ground, producing a textured cloth approximately 20 x 150 cm.
sourceNotes: Pickup stick patterning on rigid heddle looms is documented in contemporary handweaving instruction. Method is observation-led and consistent with current rigid heddle teaching practice.

content.text: sits in the warp at all times during the patterned section. When turned on its edge, it opens a pattern shed that lets the weft pass over selected threads, creating small surface
. The finished scarf measures approximately 20 x 150 cm.

items.name: Rigid heddle loom, 30 cm or wider weaving width
Warp yarn (8/2 cotton or DK wool)
Ground weft (matches warp or subtle contrast, DK weight)
Pickup stick or smooth flat ruler

content.text: Warp the loom at 8 ends per cm (20 per inch) to a 20 cm weaving width, leaving 40 cm at each end for fringe. Weave a few rows of plain tabby with scrap yarn to spread the warp evenly before the pattern begins.

Move the heddle to the down position to open the down shed. Working from right to left across the warp, slide the pickup stick under every other down-shed thread: under the first down-shed thread, over the second, under the third, and so on. Leave the stick lying flat in the warp.
Step 3: Weave the three-pick pattern repeat

The pattern repeat is three picks. Pick 1 (pickup pick): move the heddle to neutral, then turn the pickup stick on its edge to open the pattern shed. Pass the pattern weft through this shed, leaving a small arc, then beat. Pick 2: move the heddle up and pass the ground weft through the up shed. Beat. Pick 3: move the heddle down and pass the ground weft through the down shed. Beat. This three-pick repeat produces alternating spot floats across the surface.

Begin and end the scarf with 10 cm of plain tabby using the ground weft to frame the pattern section. Remove the pickup stick when weaving the border. Cut the scarf from the loom, leaving warp fringe at each end. Twist the fringe in groups of four threads and tie each group with an overhand knot 1 cm from the end of the twist to secure.

items.fix: Re-check the stick by counting: it should be under odd-numbered down-shed threads (1, 3, 5...) and over even ones. Once inserted, hold it firmly before turning it on its edge.
items.cause: Pickup stick inserted under the wrong set of down-shed threads, or the stick shifted position
items.problem: The pickup pattern is inconsistent across the width

items.fix: Use a heavier or more textured yarn for the pattern weft and beat the pickup pick slightly more gently than the plain picks.
items.cause: Pattern weft is the same weight as the ground and beaten down too firmly
items.problem: The pattern weft is barely visible on the surface

items.fix: Insert the stick under every other down-shed thread only. Floats of more than 4-5 warp threads are too long for a scarf and should be avoided.
items.cause: Floats too long: pickup stick inserted under too many consecutive threads
items.problem: The floats catch and snag during wear
